title: atriplex glauca subsp. glauca
intro: Atriplex glauca subsp. glauca is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the amaranth family, also known as the 'glasswort' due to its glassy appearance when dry. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Atriplex glauca subsp. glauca include reducing fever, treating wounds and skin conditions, and alleviating urinary problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical applications, and are often used in herbal remedies for its therapeutic properties.
